desc: PMBOK, Agile, PMI e comunicação com stakeholders.

cards.def: A hierarchical decomposition of the total project scope into manageable work packages.
cards.ex: The WBS identified 23 work packages that had been entirely overlooked in initial planning.
cards.tip: Abreviado WBS. Pilar do PMBOK. Regra 100%: captura 100% do trabalho — nem mais, nem menos.

cards.def: The longest sequence of dependent tasks that determines the minimum project completion time.
cards.ex: Any delay on the critical path would push the product launch beyond the Q3 board-mandated deadline.
cards.tip: Método CPM. Zero float no critical path. Qualquer atraso = atraso no projeto inteiro.

cards.def: A performance metric comparing planned work against actual progress and costs at a given point in time.
cards.ex: Earned value analysis revealed the project was 15% behind schedule with only 60% of budget remaining.
cards.tip: EVM: SPI = Schedule Performance Index. CPI = Cost Performance Index. CPI < 1 = acima do orçamento.

cards.def: A tangible or intangible output that must be produced to complete a project or phase.
cards.ex: The final deliverable was a fully documented API with integration guides for three enterprise clients.
cards.tip: Deliverable ≠ milestone. Deliverable = O QUÊ é entregue (produto). Milestone = QUANDO um evento-chave ocorre.

cards.def: A document capturing identified risks with their probability, impact, owners, and response strategies.
cards.ex: The risk register flagged single-supplier dependency as high-probability, triggering a dual-source strategy.
cards.tip: Ferramenta viva — atualizada em cada ciclo. Includes: probability × impact matrix, risk owner, response plan.

cards.def: Any individual, group, or organization that may affect or be affected by a project decision.
cards.ex: Identifying all stakeholders in week one prevented a major political misalignment during implementation.
cards.tip: Stakeholder ≠ shareholder. Stakeholder management é uma das competências mais valorizadas em McKinsey engagements.

cards.def: A systematic approach to transitioning people and organizations from a current state to a desired future state.
cards.ex: Without a robust change management strategy, the ERP rollout met fierce frontline resistance.
cards.tip: McKinsey usa o OHI (Organizational Health Index). Kotter 8 Steps e ADKAR são frameworks de mercado.

cards.def: A responsibility chart mapping tasks to roles: Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, Informed.
cards.ex: The RACI matrix eliminated confusion about who had final sign-off authority on design decisions.
cards.tip: R = faz o trabalho. A = dono único do resultado. C = consultado antes. I = informado depois.

cards.def: A significant, zero-duration event marking the completion of a major phase or decision point.
cards.ex: The board required monthly milestone updates to validate continued investment in the transformation.
cards.tip: Zero duration — é um ponto no tempo. Em Gantt charts: representado por diamante (◆). Usado em gate reviews.

cards.def: The approved plan against which project performance is measured, capturing scope, schedule, and cost.
cards.ex: After the client added new features, we formally re-baselined the project to reflect the expanded scope.
cards.tip: PMBOK: scope + schedule + cost baseline = Performance Measurement Baseline (PMB). Changes requerem formal approval.

cards.def: A fixed time-box in Scrum (typically 1–4 weeks) during which a committed set of work is completed.
cards.ex: Each two-week sprint concluded with a working-software demo aligned to client expectations.
cards.tip: Key events: Planning, Daily Scrum, Review, Retrospective. Key artifacts: Sprint Goal, Backlog, Increment.

cards.def: Documented knowledge from a project capturing successes, failures, and recommendations for future performance.
cards.ex: The lessons learned session became a mandatory reference document for all subsequent deployments.
cards.tip: Obrigatório no closing process group (PMBOK). Alimenta organizational process assets (OPA).
